signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Fed Hike Doubts
signals.text: GBP/USD upside momentum is driven by a 1.477% G10 performance spread as weak US Nonfarm Payrolls data forces a repricing of the Fed's terminal rate. The pair closed Friday at 1.3350, supported by a positive Economic Surprise Index (net +23) and a neutral carry profile (0bp OIS spread) that removes the traditional USD advantage. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 Jul is the catalyst for determining if the current 0.78% real rate remains sufficiently restrictive to sustain the bid.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry Advantage vs Squeeze Risk
signals.text: JPY COT LF net short -115.0k (28.4% long) creates significant short-squeeze risk as USD/JPY faces downside pressure from disappointing US data. While the +268bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the strong options put skew (RR -1.67) indicates heavy hedging demand for a move below the 161.34 Friday close. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul serves as the catalyst for a shift in the -0.52% real rate accommodative stance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — RBNZ Hike Bias vs Crowded Retail Long
signals.text: NZD/USD upside is supported by an active RBNZ hike bias and a +0.706% G10 weekly outperformance, yet faces headwinds from a 74% retail long concentration. The pair closed at 0.5712 Friday, but the -0.83% real rate remains the most accommodative in the G10, suggesting the rally relies on policy divergence rather than fundamental value. The RBNZ meeting on 8 Jul is the catalyst to validate the hike pricing and clear the retail overhang.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Extreme vs COT Alignment
signals.text: USD/CAD retail positioning is at an extreme 93% short, creating a contrarian risk for further upside despite the pair's 1.4198 Friday close. This retail bias conflicts with the CAD COT net short of -87.0k, which aligns with a negative Economic Surprise Index (net -6) and a +136bp OIS carry advantage for the USD. The BoC meeting on 15 Jul is the catalyst to resolve this positioning conflict through updated growth guidance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT LF net long +29.0k (64.9% long) creates an unwind risk if the 0.6943 Friday close fails to hold amid broader USD volatility. While the +69bp OIS carry advantage and a positive Economic Surprise Index (+8) support the AUD, the moderate options put skew (RR -0.78) suggests markets are pricing protection against a reversal. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug determines if the 4.35% hold remains sufficient to protect the long positioning.
